problem_text: Classify sum_{n=1}^infinity (-1)^(n+1)/n as absolutely convergent, conditionally convergent, or divergent.

content.label: Alternating series form
content.applies_when: the terms alternate in sign: a_n = (-1)^(n+1) b_n with b_n = 1/n > 0.

content.meaning: if b_n > 0, b_n is decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, then the alternating series sum (-1)^n b_n converges.

content.label: check b_n = 1/n is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges by the Alternating Series Test
content.action: check b_n = 1/n is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges by the Alternating Series Test
content.purpose: establish convergence of the alternating series

content.meaning: sum 1/n is the harmonic series (p = 1) and diverges.

content.label: test the series of absolute values sum |(-1)^(n+1)/n| = sum 1/n, the harmonic series, which diverges
content.action: test the series of absolute values sum |(-1)^(n+1)/n| = sum 1/n, the harmonic series, which diverges
content.purpose: decide whether the convergence is absolute

content.label: Conditional convergence
content.applies_when: the series converges but the series of absolute values diverges.

content.label: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
content.action: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
